original_abstract: Developing alkali metal-based metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) with permanent porosity remains a significant challenge due to the propensity of these metals to coordinate with solvent molecules, which often leads to framework collapse upon activation. However, their lightweight nature offers distinct gravimetric advantages for gas storage and separation. In this work, we report the synthesis of HIAM-111, a novel microporous MOF constructed entirely from sodium ions and an octacarboxylate linker. The structure is characterized by 8-connected Na₄ clusters that assemble into a unique 3²,4²,8-c topology. Notably, HIAM-111 exhibits exceptional architectural robustness and a BET surface area of 1561 m²/g, a value that represents a new benchmark for Na-based MOFs. Adsorption studies reveal that HIAM-111 is highly effective for the separation of C₂H₂/CO₂ mixtures and the direct purification of C₂H₄ from ternary C₂H₄/C₂H₂/C₂H₆ streams. These findings provide a framework for the rational design of stable, high-porosity alkali metal MOFs for industrial gas separations.
humanized_abstract: The preparation of alkali metal based alkali metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) with permanent porosity is highly desired, but hardly achieved because alkali metals can bind with large amount of solvent molecules and lead to the collapse of MOF structures after activation. MOFs based on alkali metals are still considered as potential materials for gas storage and separation applications due to the low density of alkali metals. Herein, a sodium MOF, denoted as HIAM-111, composed of sodium ions and an octacarboxylate linker, was successfully prepared and characterized with a 3²,4²,8-c topology composed of 8-connected Ni₄ clusters. HIAM-111 shows high stability and achieves a high BET surface area of 1561 m²/g, which is the highest value reported so far for sodium based MOFs. Moreover, gas adsorption experiments show that HIAM-111 could efficiently separate C₂H₂ from CO₂, and also be employed for the direct separation of pure C₂H₄ from complex ternary C₂H₄/C₂H₂/C₂H₆ mixtures, endowing it as a promising material for gas separation applications.

MOFs based on alkali metals are still considered as potential materials for gas storage and separation applications due to the low density of alkali metals. Herein, a sodium MOF, denoted as HIAM-111, composed of sodium ions and an octacarboxylate linker, was successfully prepared and characterized with a 3²,4²,8-c topology composed of 8-connected Ni₄ clusters. HIAM-111 shows high stability and achieves a high BET surface area of 1561 m²/g, which is the highest value reported so far for sodium based MOFs. Moreover, gas adsorption experiments show that HIAM-111 could efficiently separate C₂H₂ from CO₂, and also be employed for the direct separation of pure C₂H₄ from complex ternary C₂H₄/C₂H₂/C₂H₆ mixtures, endowing it as a promising material for gas separation applications.
